primary_catalyst.description: Big Tech 4 (AMZN, GOOGL, META, MSFT) earnings/capex guidance crystallized into a combined $650 BILLION 2026 AI infrastructure commitment. Amazon $200B, Alphabet $185B, Meta up to $135B, Microsoft $105B â€” roughly 60% YoY increase. Bloomberg/Fortune reporting on Feb 6 aggregated this into a single landmark figure that crushed the AI capex peak thesis. NVDA as the primary beneficiary rallied +7.87% on heavy volume.

secondary_catalysts.description: Bloomberg: 'How Much Is Big Tech Spending on AI Computing? A Staggering $650 Billion in 2026'
Fortune: 'Big Tech to Spend $650 Billion This Year as AI Race Intensifies'

force_attributions.rationale: Hyperscaler capex cycle decisively re-rated upward by $650B aggregated figure. F1 Tier 3 (major customers themselves making capex claims) = 0.7x multiplier â€” high independence, material revenue visibility
Short covering and systematic re-engagement; CTA buy programs triggered

notes: CRITICAL regime-changing move â€” largest single-day move YTD at +7.87%, z=+4.25. The $650B figure became the canonical anchor for 2026 AI capex thesis. This single narrative reversal undid 3 weeks of AI-peak worry.
